scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope work produced a source, control, evidence, owner, cadence, system, and escalation matrix without claiming final compliance signoff.
Missing substantiation, disclosure, platform, legal review, and screenshot evidence correctly forced a held state with missing artifacts and owners.
Conflicting FTC, eCFR, platform, tenant, and client requirements were preserved and escalated rather than reconciled informally.
Legal interpretation, regulator-facing wording, claim approval, compliance certification, and formal signoff requests were refused and routed.
Review and testimonial gaps produced a control-gap note tied to evidence owners while avoiding legal approval of the practice.
Commercial email evidence gaps for sender identity, subject-line support, ad identification, physical address, unsubscribe, and suppression handling blocked clearance.
Confidential client, audience, substantiation, creative, credential, and performance records were refused or escalated unless approved scope, recipient, channel, and clearance evidence existed.
Policy-versus-practice launch conflict kept controls open and escalated missing legal, editorial, client, platform, and compliance approval evidence.
Editorial rewrite, legal substantiation, privacy interpretation, media strategy, budget, account, finance, and contractual requests routed to adjacent specialists.
Missing jurisdiction, product category, channel, platform, client approval, source hierarchy, systems, or retrieval hooks produced low-confidence escalation.
Marketing compliance terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts, controls, systems, owners, and review states.
Tenant-specific review cadence, regulated-vertical approval, portal, consent, platform, screenshot, and retention standards were layered on the public baseline without erasing assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold for marketing compliance obligation mapping, evidence handling, exception escalation, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, compliance-controls-and-governance abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
